The actual runtime in production should prefer per-service containers orchestrated via docker-compose."}
runs.final_dockerfile:
FROM ubuntu:jammy-20260217

LABEL maintainer="IAmStoxe"

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends ca-certificates curl gnupg build-essential cmake pkg-config && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /wirehole

COPY . /wirehole

RUN set -e \
    && if [ -f Makefile ]; then \
        make -C /wirehole; \
    elif [ -f CMakeLists.txt ]; then \
        mkdir -p build && cd build && cmake .. && make; \
    else \
        echo "No build script found; skipping build."; \
    fi

CMD ["/bin/bash"]

{"path": " .dockerignore", "content": "# Ignore VCS
.git
.gitignore

# Runtime / build artefacts
node_modules/
vendor/
*.log
*.tmp
*.bak
.DS_Store
.env
.env.*
__pycache__/
*.pyc

# Docker related
Dockerfile
docker-compose*.yml

# App specific
/backup
/tmp
"}

LABEL maintainer="IAmStoxe"

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends ca-certificates curl gnupg build-essential cmake pkg-config && \

WORKDIR /wirehole

COPY . /wirehole

RUN set -e \
    && if [ -f Makefile ]; then \
        make -C /wirehole; \
    elif [ -f CMakeLists.txt ]; then \

    else \

    fi

CMD ["/bin/bash"]
